title: Hedge
author: Jane Delury
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Hedge" book by Jane Delury?
Options:
A. Fell in love. Met his vet. Fell in love. And lived quietly ever after, Maud said. Yeah, I guess. Until this bigmouthed garden historian came along. Esperanza reappeared, its silver roofs glinting. Clark waited at the trailhead with his muzzle resting on his front paws. You dont miss sculpting? Maud said. You dont miss that feeling? It doesnt matter if I miss it, Alice said. Clark nosed her leg, and she scratched his ears. You do, Maud thought, watching her still face. Thats why those barn windows are whitewashed. But you dont know how to have it without all the rest.
B. Developed feelings. Encountered his veterinarian. Developed feelings. And lived calmly ever after, Maud said. Yeah, I assume. Until this loudmouthed garden chronicler arrived. Esperanza re-emerged, its silver rooftops glistening. Clark lingered at the trail entrance with his snout resting on his front paws. You do not yearn for sculpting? Maud said. You do not miss that sensation? It does not matter if I long for it, Alice said. Clark nudged her leg, and she scratched his ears. You do, Maud thought, observing her still face. That is why those barn windows are whitewashed. But you do not know how to have it without all the rest.
C. Became enamored. Encountered his pet physician. Became enamored. And lived serenely ever after, Maud said. Yeah, I presume. Until this loud-mouthed garden chronicler arrived. Esperanza emerged again, its silver roofs sparkling. Clark lingered at the trail beginning with his snout on his front paws. You do not yearn for sculpting? Maud said. You do not miss that feeling? It does not matter if I yearn for it, Alice said. Clark nudged her leg, and she scratched his ears. You do, Maud thought, observing her motionless face. That is why those barn windows are whitewashed. But you do not know how to have it without all the rest.
D. Fell for someone. Met his animal doctor. Fell for someone. And existed calmly ever after, Maud said. Yeah, I reckon. Until this big-mouthed garden historian showed up. Esperanza came back, its silver roofs shining. Clark hung around at the trail start with his muzzle on his front paws. You don't pine for sculpting? Maud said. You don't miss that feeling? It doesn't matter if I pine for it, Alice said. Clark bumped her leg, and she scratched his ears. You do, Maud thought, looking at her still face. That's why those barn windows are whitewashed. But you don't know how to have it without all the rest.  
Answer:
